answer: The dog saw the bad goat.
reasoning: Mva = dog (subject), imwi = saw (verb), ntaw = goat (object), nebiba = bad (adjective modifying goat)

answer: The little man read the good book.

answer: Keto ke kebil ketang mekan mebil.
reasoning: Keto = snakes, ke = possessive marker, kebil = field, ketang = read (ke-class verb from otang), mekan = books (me-class plural), mebil = big
ipa = pig (from nchunga ipa = two pigs), lemo = one (universal form for 'one' seen in ipyao lemo)
meni = tree (from mete meni = four trees), lemo = one
besa = bone (from befwa besa = three bones), lemo = one
nchunga = two (from nchunga ipa = two pigs), ipyao = shoulder (from ipyao lemo = one shoulder)
